msserver2019standard.label: Windows Server 2019
msserver2019standard.action: Apply cumulative update KB5082123 via WSUS or Windows Update

alternatives.label: OOB Apr 19
alternatives.action: Apply OOB KB5091573 on domain controllers in multi-domain forests with PAM enabled (fixes LSASS crash/restart loop introduced by KB5082123)
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ OOB update — only required if you hit the LSASS crash issue on DCs in multi-domain PAM environments. Supersedes KB5082123.

alternatives.label: Mar 2026 CU
alternatives.action: Apply March 2026 cumulative update as a rollback target if April CU causes issues
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ Pinning to an older CU leaves April 2026 CVEs unpatched. Use only as a temporary rollback.

alternatives.label: WSUS Catalog
alternatives.action: Approve and deploy via internal WSUS server according to change management process

msserver2012r2standard.label: Windows Server 2012 R2
msserver2012r2standard.action: Apply Monthly Rollup KB5082126 via WSUS (ESU license required; SSU KB5079233 prereq)
msserver2012r2standard.warning: ⚠️ End of Support Oct 2023. ESU required. Final EOL: October 13, 2026 — plan migration.

alternatives.label: Security-Only
alternatives.action: Apply security-only update KB5082125 via WSUS for minimal change footprint (ESU license required)
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ Security-only skips reliability/quality fixes in the Monthly Rollup. EOL: October 13, 2026 — plan migration to Server 2022.

alternatives.label: Migrate → 2022
alternatives.action: Plan in-place upgrade or migration to Windows Server 2022 before October 2026 EOL
alternatives.warning: 🚫 Final EOL: October 13, 2026. No further patches after that date even with ESU. Migration is the only long-term fix.

msserver2016standard.label: Windows Server 2016
msserver2016standard.action: Apply cumulative update KB5082198 via WSUS or Windows Update
msserver2016standard.warning: ⚠️ Server 2016 mainstream support ends January 2027. Begin migration planning to Server 2022 now.

alternatives.label: OOB Apr 19
alternatives.action: Apply OOB KB5091572 on domain controllers in multi-domain forests with PAM enabled (fixes LSASS crash/restart loop introduced by KB5082198)
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ OOB update — only required if you hit the LSASS crash issue on DCs in multi-domain PAM environments. Supersedes KB5082198.

alternatives.label: WSUS Catalog
alternatives.action: Approve and deploy KB5082198 via internal WSUS server according to change management process

alternatives.label: Migrate → 2022
alternatives.action: Consider migration to Windows Server 2022 (mainstream support until Oct 2031)
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ Server 2016 mainstream support ends January 2027. Begin migration planning now.

msserver2022standard.label: Windows Server 2022
msserver2022standard.action: Apply cumulative update KB5082142 via WSUS or Windows Update

alternatives.label: WSUS Catalog
alternatives.action: Approve and deploy KB5082142 via internal WSUS server according to change management process

alternatives.label: OOB Apr 19
alternatives.action: Apply out-of-band update KB5091575 if your environment uses multi-domain forests with PAM enabled (fixes LSASS crash on domain controllers)
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ OOB update — only required if you hit the LSASS crash issue on domain controllers in multi-domain PAM environments. Supersedes KB5082142.

.NET Framework (All Versions).label: .NET Framework
.NET Framework (All Versions).action: Upgrade from 4.7.x / 4.8 → 4.8.1 via Windows Update or WSUS (April 2026 servicing wave)
.NET Framework (All Versions).warning: ⚠️ Versions 4.7, 4.7.1, 4.7.2 are outdated. 4.8.1 is the final .NET Framework release.

alternatives.label: 4.8 Security Patch
alternatives.action: Apply .NET 4.8 security servicing update if upgrading to 4.8.1 is not yet approved
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ 4.8 still receives security patches but 4.8.1 is the final supported release. Schedule the upgrade.

alternatives.label: Update Catalog
alternatives.action: Look up the exact April 2026 .NET 4.8.1 cumulative KB for your OS in the Microsoft Update Catalog before deployment
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ .NET 4.8.1 KB number varies by host OS. Verify in Update Catalog and approve via WSUS.

alternatives.label: Migrate → .NET 8
alternatives.action: Migrate applications to .NET 8 LTS for modern runtime support and future security updates
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ .NET Framework 4.x is legacy. New features and performance improvements are only in .NET 5+.

SQL Server 2019 (All Components).label: SQL Server 2019
SQL Server 2019 (All Components).action: Install CU32 + GDR (KB5084816). CU32 is the final CU; April 2026 security GDR applied on top.
SQL Server 2019 (All Components).warning: ⚠️ Mainstream Support ended Feb 28, 2025. Extended Support until Jan 8, 2030.

alternatives.label: GDR Only
alternatives.action: Apply GDR-only patch for security fixes without cumulative update changes (lower change risk)
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ GDR-only skips reliability and feature fixes in CU32. Use if CU testing is not yet complete.

alternatives.label: Migrate → 2022
alternatives.action: Plan upgrade to SQL Server 2022 (mainstream support until Jan 2033)
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ Mainstream Support ended Feb 2025. Begin planning upgrade to SQL Server 2022.

SQL Server 2017 (All Components).label: SQL Server 2017
SQL Server 2017 (All Components).action: Install CU31 + GDR (KB5084818). April 2026 security GDR on top of final CU.
SQL Server 2017 (All Components).warning: ⚠️ Mainstream Support ended Oct 2022. Extended Support until Oct 2027.

alternatives.label: GDR Only
alternatives.action: Apply GDR-only patch for security fixes with minimal change impact
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ GDR-only skips CU31 reliability and feature fixes. CU31 + GDR is recommended.

alternatives.label: Migrate → 2022
alternatives.action: Plan upgrade to SQL Server 2022 — mainstream support until January 2033
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ Extended Support ends Oct 2027. Upgrade planning should begin now.

SQL Server 2016 (All Components).label: SQL Server 2016
SQL Server 2016 (All Components).action: Install SP3 + GDR (KB5084821). No further CUs will be released for SQL Server 2016.
SQL Server 2016 (All Components).warning: ⚠️ Mainstream Support ended July 2021. Extended Support ends July 11, 2026 — migration is urgent.

alternatives.label: Migrate → 2022
alternatives.action: Plan upgrade to SQL Server 2022 (mainstream support until Jan 2033)
alternatives.warning: 🚫 Extended Support ends July 2026. This is the only long-term fix.

alternatives.label: Migrate → 2019
alternatives.action: Upgrade to SQL Server 2019 as an interim step if 2022 migration is not yet feasible
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ SQL Server 2019 mainstream support ended Feb 2025. Target 2022 for long-term support.

Internet Explorer (All Versions).label: Internet Explorer
Internet Explorer (All Versions).action: Remove IE and migrate to Microsoft Edge (Chromium)
Internet Explorer (All Versions).warning: 🚫 Internet Explorer is fully retired. No security patches. Immediate action required.

alternatives.label: IE Mode (Edge)
alternatives.action: Deploy Edge with IE Mode for legacy intranet sites that require IE rendering engine
alternatives.warning: ⚠️ IE Mode in Edge maintains the IE rendering engine for compatibility. Full IE removal is still the goal.
